intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

compositional_truth_check.details: The registry indicates four distinct plant entities (P1-P4). Visual evidence confirms only the presence of P3 (Pothos with rabbit anchor) and the yellow pot (P1). P2 and P4 are entirely absent or reduced to non-viable soil substrate.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_String_of_Nickels: Present (Yellow Pot); no visible foliage, soil appears dormant/barren.
inventory_reconciliation.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss; no visible plant matter.
inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Present (Black Pot); single viable sprout/leaf visible.
inventory_reconciliation.P4_Silver_Guest: Systemic Loss; no visible plant matter.

plant_audit.P1: Barren soil, no signs of String of Nickels growth.
plant_audit.P3: Single green shoot emerging from soil, showing slight vertical elongation over the 5-day period.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all visible pots.
biome_observations.debris: White granular material (likely perlite or mineral deposits) present on the surface of P3.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: No fungal blooms or incidental weeds detected.

temporal_deltas.t-3_to_t-2: P3 shows a slight increase in the curvature of the emerging sprout.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_current: Sprout in P3 has stabilized in its current posture; no further growth or decay observed.

visual_health_inference.P3: Stressed but viable; the single sprout is the only sign of life in the entire biome.

anomalies: Total absence of P2 and P4 specimens.
P1 appears to be an empty vessel despite registry status.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is in a state of severe decline. Of the four registered specimens, only one (P3) exhibits any biological activity in the form of a single, slow-growing sprout. The yellow pot (P1) and the black pots associated with P2/P4 are effectively barren, suggesting a catastrophic failure of the indoor biome or a lack of viable propagation material. The environment is stable but largely devoid of the expected botanical diversity.
